cases.description: Extract prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens from a non-streaming JSON response body containing a top-level 'usage' object
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Scenario: Non-streaming LLM response intercepted

cases.description: Return None / skip persistence when the response body is valid JSON but has no 'usage' key
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Requirement: Proxy Interception (guard missing usage)

cases.description: Return None / skip persistence when the response body is not valid JSON
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Requirement: Proxy Interception (malformed body guard)

cases.description: Buffer multiple SSE data chunks per flow.id and extract usage from the final chunk that carries a 'usage' object
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Scenario: Streaming response — usage in final chunk

cases.description: When the final SSE chunk has no 'usage' key: log a warning and do NOT persist any token event row
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Scenario: Streaming response — no usage in final chunk

cases.description: Release the per-flow SSE buffer from sse_buffers dict after stream completion (no memory leak)
cases.spec: token-usage-export: Requirement: Proxy Overhead (SSE buffer released after completion)

cases.description: Agent detection: X-Agent-Name header present → its value is used as agent label (highest priority)
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Requirement: Agent Detection (X-Agent-Name priority)

cases.description: Agent detection: no X-Agent-Name header, User-Agent contains 'opencode' → agent = 'opencode'
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Scenario: Known agent via User-Agent

cases.description: Agent detection: no X-Agent-Name header, User-Agent contains 'claude' or 'claude-code' → agent = 'claude-code'
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Requirement: Agent Detection

cases.description: Agent detection: no X-Agent-Name, unrecognized User-Agent → agent = 'unknown'
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Scenario: Unknown agent fallback

cases.description: Agent detection: neither X-Agent-Name nor User-Agent header present → agent = 'unknown', no crash
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Scenario: Unknown agent fallback (no headers at all)

cases.description: Header extraction: X-Project header present → project field populated in event dict
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Requirement: Agent Detection (nullable header fields)

cases.description: Header extraction: X-Phase header present → phase field populated in event dict
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Requirement: Agent Detection (nullable header fields)

cases.description: Header extraction: X-Session-ID header present → session_id field populated in event dict
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Requirement: Agent Detection (nullable header fields)

cases.description: Header extraction: X-Request-Id header present → request_id field populated in event dict
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Requirement: Agent Detection (nullable header fields)

cases.description: Header extraction: all nullable headers absent → corresponding fields are NULL, no crash
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Requirement: Agent Detection (nullable headers persist as NULL)

cases.description: storage.py: insert_event() writes a complete token_events row; verify all 15 schema columns via SELECT
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Scenario: Row inserted on successful capture

cases.description: storage.py: after TokenStorage.__init__, PRAGMA journal_mode returns 'wal'
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Requirement: SQLite Persistence (WAL mode)

cases.description: storage.py: insert_event() raises an internal error (e.g. table dropped mid-session) → error is caught and logged; no exception propagates to the caller
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Scenario: DB write error does not crash proxy

cases.description: export.py: given a pre-seeded DB with 3 rows, --format jsonl outputs exactly 3 valid JSON objects, one per line, each with all 15 schema fields
cases.spec: token-usage-export: Scenario: Full JSONL export succeeds

cases.description: export.py: given rows where some session_id values are NULL, --format csv outputs a header row + data rows with empty strings for NULL cells
cases.spec: token-usage-export: Scenario: Full CSV export succeeds

cases.description: export.py: when the database file does not exist, the script exits with code 1 and prints to stderr
cases.spec: token-usage-export: Scenario: Missing database exits with error

cases.description: Integration (document-only): Full flow — agent sends HTTPS request through running mitmdump → addon hooks response → mock LLM API returns a non-streaming JSON payload → exactly one row is written to SQLite with correct field values
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Scenario: Non-streaming LLM response intercepted (end-to-end)

cases.description: Integration (document-only): SSE full flow — mitmdump running, server sends N SSE chunks with usage only in the final chunk → SSE buffer accumulates all chunks → exactly one row persisted after stream end
cases.spec: token-usage-capture: Scenario: Streaming response — usage in final chunk (end-to-end)

cases.description: Static: verify token_monitor.py, storage.py, export.py use parameterized SQL (no f-string or %-format SQL interpolation)
cases.spec: design.md#secure-development-design: parameterized queries by design

cases.description: Static: verify no request/response body, prompt content, or API key is persisted in SQLite schema columns or logged to stderr
cases.spec: design.md#secure-development-design: no sensitive data stored

cases.description: Static: verify storage.py uses pathlib.Path for all file path operations (Windows portability)
cases.spec: token-usage-export: Requirement: Portability

cases.description: Static: verify export.py uses pathlib.Path for all file path operations (Windows portability)
cases.spec: token-usage-export: Requirement: Portability
